entities.name: FISH
entities.description: The target animal for the feeds produced in the study

entities.name: CRICKET
entities.description: Acheta domesticus, used as a source for meal substitution

entities.name: BLACK SOLDIER FLY
entities.description: Hermetia illucens, used as a source for meal substitution

entities.name: FISH MEAL
entities.description: An integral component of fish feeds that is being substituted

entities.name: ADULT CRICKET MEAL
entities.description: Meal derived from adult crickets used to substitute fish meal

entities.name: BLACK SOLDIER FLY MEAL
entities.description: Meal derived from black soldier fly larvae used to substitute fish meal

entities.name: EXTRUDED FISH FEEDS
entities.description: The processed fish feed product analyzed for mineral content and leaching

entities.name: MINERALS
entities.description: Nutrients analyzed in the extruded fish feeds

entities.name: PHOSPHORUS
entities.description: A mineral whose levels increased with fish meal substitution

entities.name: POTASSIUM
entities.description: A mineral whose levels increased with fish meal substitution

entities.name: IRON
entities.description: A mineral whose levels decreased with fish meal substitution

entities.name: SODIUM
entities.description: A mineral whose levels decreased with fish meal substitution

entities.name: MAGNESIUM
entities.description: A mineral whose levels varied depending on whether ACM or BSFM was used

entities.name: COPPER
entities.description: A mineral not greatly influenced by fish meal substitution

entities.name: ZINC
entities.description: A mineral not greatly influenced by fish meal substitution

entities.name: MANGANESE
entities.description: A mineral not greatly influenced by fish meal substitution

entities.name: FEED MOISTURE CONTENT
entities.description: The moisture levels (20% and 30%) studied for their effect on minerals

entities.name: LEACHING EFFECT
entities.description: The loss of minerals from the feed pellets

entities.name: SUBSTITUTION
entities.description: The process of replacing fish meal with ACM or BSFM at levels of 0, 25, 50, and 75%
